patches.find: we retract our earlier characterization of FSF as empirical.
patches.replace: we retract our earlier characterization of FSF as empirical. The dome's own model evolution log records that its pre-v45 predecessor 'Incorrectly predicted 2024 eclipse magnitudes identically across all latitudes'; FSF = H(r)/r was introduced specifically to repair that failure. A genuine prospective prediction would have specified the station-to-station scaling before the 2024 observations arrived.
patches.rationale: ISS-1461: Add dome's own pre-v45 failure admission from raw-text/06-evolution.txt. Strengthens argument that FSF is retroactive repair, not prediction.

patches.find: The dome derives the station-to-station FSF ratio from its own geometry but not the absolute magnitude, which is imported from the 2017 BOU observation and reused as the 2026 baseline input
patches.replace: The dome derives the station-to-station FSF ratio from its own geometry but provides no absolute-magnitude prediction for 2024 — the observed 9-station pattern matches what Chapman Sq physics predicts independently. The FSF absolute magnitude is imported from the 2017 BOU observation and reused as the 2026 baseline input
patches.rationale: ISS-1462: Reframe finding to lead with WIN-025-relevant critique (2024 observation) rather than 2026 architecture detail. Curmudgeon c3 H2.

patches.find: WIN-025 shares this mechanism with WIN-010 and WIN-068 — counting the same eclipse geomagnetic observation three times inflates the WIN count without adding independent evidence.
patches.replace: Even after conceding FSF = H(r)/r is genuine geometry, the mechanism that produces the observed magnitudes (Chapman Sq current disruption) is the same across all three WINs; the dome counts one physical process three times at different aggregation scales and counts each repetition as independent confirmation.
patches.rationale: ISS-1464: Tighten triple-count sentence to stand on its own post-EXP-229 FSF concession. Curmudgeon c3 H4.

patches.find: so any fair-audit Fail-clause inherits the ambiguity below
patches.replace: so any Chapman-language fair-audit Fail-clause inherits the ambiguity below
patches.rationale: ISS-1465: Scope the overclaim. Ambiguity is specific to Fail-clauses framed against ionospheric Sq baseline, not all fair-audit Fail-clauses. Curmudgeon SEC-8b.3 c6 H1.

patches.find: Giacomin & Levinson 2015, Transportation Planning and Technology
patches.replace: Giacomin & Levinson 2015, Environment and Planning B: Planning and Design
patches.rationale: ISS-1467: Correct journal name. 'Road network circuity in metropolitan areas' was published in Environment and Planning B, vol. 42 no. 6 (2015), not Transportation Planning and Technology. Citation failure flagged by curmudgeon c4.

patches.find: producing sub-1% mean-ratio prediction errors against observed road distances with a single free parameter, versus V13's ≥4 free parameters for 6.2%.
patches.replace: producing sub-1% mean-ratio prediction errors against observed road distances with a single free parameter (V13's same-hemisphere mean error on this same metric is 10.2%, with ≥4 free parameters).
patches.rationale: ISS-1468: Fix statistic mismatch. Previous comparison used V13's 6.2% cross-equatorial RMSE vs terrain models' mean-ratio error — not apples-to-apples. V13's 10.2% is the same-hemisphere mean error on the comparable metric. Curmudgeon c4 H2.

patches.find: every other station's predicted delta_Z is a 2017 BOU number scaled by a geometric ratio.
patches.replace: every other station's predicted delta_Z is the 2017 BOU magnitude scaled by the station's obscuration coverage and its FSF = H(r)/r geometric ratio.
patches.rationale: ISS-1470: Add coverage term to match the 3-factor equation (delta_Z = baseline x coverage x FSF) adopted by the c10 finding patch. Curmudgeon c11 H2.

patches.find: all invoke the same ionospheric Sq mechanism at three different aggregation levels.
patches.replace: all cite observations explained by the same ionospheric Sq mechanism at three different aggregation levels.
patches.rationale: ISS-1471: Remove subject ambiguity — 'invoke' is ambiguous (dome invokes? we invoke?). 'Cite observations explained by' is unambiguous. Curmudgeon c11 H3.
ISS-1472: Dome does attach a dome-derived mechanism claim; what it lacks is a quantitative prediction. Adding 'numerical' removes the over-broad characterization. Curmudgeon c11 H4.
